reason_for_revision: User noticed that the final USP comment theme chart put sound and aesthetics at the top, while chapter 04/05 conclusions did not treat sound/aesthetics as the main product-planning direction.

diagnosis.section_04_sample_role: Chapter 04 used Sorftime product_reviews and product_detail evidence to summarize user tasks, scenes, journeys, and needs. Its evidence ledger stores structured summaries rather than the raw full review text, so it is not a frequency-statistics artifact.
diagnosis.section_05_sample_role: Chapter 05 mainly used product detail, traffic terms, Walmart/TikTok/official-site/frontstage evidence. It is a marketing and channel analysis, not a review-theme frequency analysis.
diagnosis.v09_v13_sample_role: The USP review section used SellerSprite review collection with 395 effective reviews, stratified by ASIN and star bucket. Hatch and Odokee contributed 208/395 effective reviews, which naturally elevated sound, routine, and bedside-aesthetic themes.
diagnosis.tagging_caveat: Theme counts are multi-label mentions. One long review can count toward sound, aesthetics, sunrise light, routine, controls, setup pain, and build quality at the same time. Counts do not equal first purchase reason.

revision_principle.keep_evidence_frequency: Show comment theme frequency as evidence of what users often mention.
revision_principle.add_strategy_weight: Add a separate USP strategic-weight view that filters themes through purchase impulse, first-glance visibility, low-price-war avoidance, company capability fit, architecture traceability, and testability.
revision_principle.do_not_overwrite_human_choice: The page can recommend a USP hypothesis but cannot mark it as the user's final selection.

data_integrity.new_claims: Only explanatory and strategic-weight inference was added.
data_integrity.inference_labeling: USP strategic weights are labeled as product-planning inference requiring human review and prototype/frontstage validation.
